_readme: DRAFT plain-English district summaries for the address lookup card. Written from general knowledge of the Indy Consolidated Zoning Ordinance — EVERY ENTRY NEEDS AICP REVIEW before public launch. 'housing' = dwelling types generally permitted by right (variances, commitments, and overlay standards can change everything). Keep each string short; the card is small.
_disclaimer: Summary only — not a zoning determination. Verify with DMD staff or the Consolidated Zoning and Subdivision Ordinance.

D-A.name: Dwelling Agricultural
D-A.housing: Single-family on large acreage; agricultural uses
D-A.summary: Rural holding district — farmland and very low-density homes.

D-3.name: Dwelling Three
D-3.summary: The workhorse single-family district across much of the old city.

D-4.name: Dwelling Four
D-4.housing: Single-family on small lots; two-family in limited cases

D-5.name: Dwelling Five
D-5.summary: Compact urban residential — doubles legal; the classic streetcar-era fabric district.

D-5II.name: Dwelling Five-Two
D-5II.summary: Urban infill variant of D-5 with tighter dimensional standards.

D-6.name: Dwelling Six
D-6.summary: Low-density multifamily — townhomes, small apartment buildings.

D-6II.name: Dwelling Six-Two
D-6II.summary: Attached and small multifamily at urban scale.

D-7.name: Dwelling Seven
D-7.summary: Apartment district — walk-ups and garden apartments.

D-8.name: Dwelling Eight
D-8.summary: High-density apartments; the most permissive residential district.

D-P.name: Dwelling Planned
D-P.summary: Planned unit development — what's allowed is whatever the recorded plan says. Look up the DP case.

MU-4.name: Mixed-Use Four
MU-4.summary: The most intense mixed-use district outside the CBD.

C-1.name: Commercial One (Office Buffer)
C-1.summary: Office buffer — low-intensity offices between neighborhoods and busier corridors.

C-3.name: Commercial Three
C-3.housing: Limited; upper-floor dwellings in some cases

C-5.name: Commercial Five
C-5.summary: General commercial, heavier retail and service uses.

C-S.name: Commercial Special
C-S.summary: Special commercial — governed by recorded commitments from its approval. Look up the case.

CBD-1.name: Central Business District One
CBD-1.summary: Regional Center core — the most permissive district in the county.

CBD-S.name: CBD Special
CBD-S.summary: Special CBD district — see recorded commitments.

I-1.name: Industrial One
I-1.summary: Light industrial — flex, warehousing, light manufacturing.

SU-*.name: Special Use
SU-*.summary: Special use district (churches, schools, utilities, etc.) — tied to the specific approved use.

verdicts.U: The Land Use Plan already calls for MORE than current zoning allows here — a rezoning toward the plan is asking for consistency, not a favor.
verdicts.UG: Undeveloped fringe: the plan intends suburban development the current agricultural zoning doesn't yet allow.
verdicts.O: Current zoning allows more than the plan recommends here.
verdicts.C: Zoning and the Land Use Plan are broadly in agreement here.
verdicts.C*: Intensity matches the plan, but the district is auto-oriented where the plan wants walkable form.
verdicts.X: The zoning's use family isn't what the plan contemplates here.
verdicts.ctx: Planned/special district — what's allowed depends on the specific recorded approval.
